message.subject: Result Note: Lipid panel within target on current dose
message.body: Reviewed lipid panel from this morning. LDL 78, HDL 52, triglycerides 134 — well within secondary-prevention targets on rosuvastatin 20 mg. No medication change. Continue current regimen and recheck at her next routine visit in 6 months. No callback needed.

message.subject: Result Note: BNP 142 — stable, no change
message.body: BNP 142 today, up from 118 in February but well below his historical decompensated range. Weight stable per his last MyChart message. No diuretic adjustment. Hold current torsemide dose and recheck BNP at his June follow-up. No callback required.

message.subject: Result Note: A1c 7.6 — please titrate Jardiance per protocol
message.body: A1c trended from 7.1 in November to 7.6 today on metformin 1000 BID with good dietary effort. She tolerates Jardiance 10 mg without GU side effects. Please titrate to 25 mg daily and recheck A1c in 3 months. Standard hand-off: nurse to call patient and place pharmacy send.

message.subject: Result Note: INR 4.2 — please call patient today
message.body: INR 4.2 today, last week was 2.6. No bleeding reported per phlebotomy intake. Hold tonight's warfarin dose. Recheck INR in 48 hours. Please call the patient this afternoon to confirm hold and arrange recheck. No vitamin K unless symptomatic.

message.subject: Result Note: Lipid panel stable on atorvastatin
message.body: LDL 92, HDL 41, TG 158. Slight uptick from the November panel but still within his post-MI tolerance band on atorva 40. No change. Recheck in 6 months.

message.subject: Result Note: BNP 88 — stable on current diuretic
message.body: BNP 88, in line with her last three quarters. Continues to walk her usual loop without dyspnea per chart. No change.

message.subject: Result Note: INR 2.4 — in range, continue current dose
message.body: INR 2.4, comfortably in her 2.0–3.0 range on warfarin 5 mg daily Mon/Wed/Fri and 2.5 mg Tue/Thu/Sat/Sun. Recheck in 4 weeks.

message.subject: Result Note: K+ 6.1 — please act today
message.body: Potassium 6.1 on today's BMP, up from 4.8 last week. Hold spironolactone immediately and recheck BMP within 24 hours. Confirm no NSAIDs. Call him this afternoon. If repeat is still >5.5, ED referral.

message.subject: Result Note: A1c 6.8 — at goal
message.body: A1c 6.8, at her individualized goal. Continue metformin and Ozempic. No change. Recheck in 3 months.

message.subject: Result Note: Lipid panel — at goal post-statin titration
message.body: LDL 64 after the recent uptitration to rosuvastatin 40. HDL 49, TG 121. Excellent. No change.

message.subject: Result Note: BNP 1240 + new orthopnea — please call same day
message.body: BNP 1240, up from 380 in March. Patient endorsed new 3-pillow orthopnea on phone screen this morning. Please call her this afternoon, increase torsemide to 40 mg today, daily weights, and schedule a same-week clinic visit. ED if she develops dyspnea at rest.

message.subject: Result Note: INR 1.6 — subtherapeutic, increase weekly dose
message.body: INR 1.6 today, down from 2.3 last check. Increase weekly warfarin total by 10% — round to nearest half-pill. Recheck INR in 1 week.

message.subject: Result Note: INR 2.0 — bottom of range, small uptitration
message.body: INR 2.0 today on warfarin 5 mg daily. Trended down over the past three checks. Increase weekly dose by 5% — round to nearest half-pill. Recheck INR in 1 week. Standard coumadin clinic letter.

message.subject: Result Note: A1c 9.1 — please push titration this week
message.body: A1c 9.1, up from 8.4 in February. She's been on metformin 1000 BID + Trulicity 0.75. Increase Trulicity to 1.5 mg weekly and add basal insulin teaching. Nurse to call this week, schedule diabetes educator, and recheck A1c in 8 weeks.

message.subject: Handoff: nurse follow-up on caffeine-related palpitations
message.body: He messaged MyChart describing 2–3 brief palpitation episodes after morning coffee — no syncope, no chest pain. Nurse to advise reducing caffeine, log episodes for one week, and call back if frequency increases. Reply via MyChart in plain language.

message.subject: Handoff: device-nurse follow-up + sacubitril uptitration teaching
message.body: Recent BMP shows stable creatinine 1.0, K+ 4.1. NT-proBNP unchanged at 720. Move sacubitril/valsartan from 24/26 BID to 49/51 BID next refill. Nurse to teach the dose change, place pharmacy send, and book a 4-week recheck.

message.subject: Phone msg + BP log: home BP averaging 142/86, asking next step
message.body: Patient dropped off his home BP log and called this morning asking for guidance. Mr. Hardenkvist reviewed in passing and noted: 'Numbers consistent with stage-1 — let's add lisinopril 5 mg and recheck in 4 weeks. Have nurse call to start.' Routing to your queue.
message.epic_routing: Front Desk Triage — Phone + BP Log

message.subject: Phone msg: patient-reported home BP 195/110, no symptoms
message.body: Patient called the front desk at 2 PM reporting home BP 195/110, repeated 10 minutes apart on her validated cuff. No headache, no vision change, no chest pain. Typing the message up and routing to clinical immediately.

message.subject: Phone msg: side effect concern on metoprolol (fatigue)
message.body: Patient called this morning saying he's been feeling fatigued since starting metoprolol three weeks ago. Heart rate at rest on his home monitor is in the low 50s. Asking the nurse to call back — typed up the message and routing.

message.subject: Forwarded BP log: 14-day home log dropped off at front desk
message.body: Patient brought in his 14-day home BP log this morning. Averages look acceptable (132/80) but a few outliers above 150 systolic. No phone screen done. Routing to nursing to review and decide if a callback is warranted.
message.epic_routing: Front Desk Triage — BP Log Drop-off

message.subject: Refill request: HCTZ — pharmacy fax, no refills left
message.body: Walgreens faxed in a refill request for hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg. Last refill in February with no refills remaining on the active order. Routing to nursing — please verify and re-send the prescription if appropriate.

message.subject: Refill request: atorvastatin 40 mg (Walgreens fax)
message.body: Walgreens faxed a refill request for his atorvastatin 40 mg. Last visit was October. Routing to nursing — please verify next clinic visit timing and send the refill if appropriate, otherwise add to pre-visit task list.

message.subject: Refill request: metoprolol — patient asking about dose change
message.body: Patient called the front desk asking for a metoprolol refill but mentioned that her recent message to Mr. Hardenkvist suggested a dose decrease from 50 BID to 25 BID. Need clinical confirmation before sending the refill. Routing.

message.subject: MDINR fax received: INR 3.0
message.body: MDINR fax came in this morning with INR 3.0 on his standing draw. Top of his 2.0–3.0 range on warfarin 7.5 mg daily. Routing to coumadin clinic for review. No bleeding noted on the fax cover.

message.subject: MDINR fax received: INR 2.7
message.body: MDINR fax came in for his standing twice-monthly draw. INR 2.7, in his 2.0–3.0 range. Routing to coumadin clinic for the standard letter and re-check schedule.

message.subject: Phone triage: patient called central line about leg swelling
message.body: Patient called the central call line tonight asking about new ankle swelling over the past 3 days. No dyspnea, no weight gain reported. Confirmed Phelps cardiology is her clinic. Routing to nursing for callback and clinical assessment.

message.subject: MyChart escalated: chest pain x 30 min, sweating, not relieved by rest
message.body: Patient sent MyChart message: 'I have been having chest pressure for about half an hour, pretty bad, and I'm sweating. It's not getting better when I sit down. My husband is here.' Triage flagged STAT. Patient has known CAD with prior LAD stent (2023). Please call immediately and direct to call 911 / nearest ED.

message.subject: MyChart: question about taking metoprolol with morning coffee
message.body: Hi nurse — quick question. I started metoprolol last week and my mornings are usually coffee then breakfast then meds. Is it OK to take metoprolol after coffee or should I separate them? Just want to make sure I'm doing this right. Thanks.

message.subject: Phone: patient called direct line, lightheadedness on standing x 4 days
message.body: Patient called the direct line this evening reporting lightheadedness when standing up the past 4 days. Resolves after a few seconds — she denies actual syncope or falls but the description is consistent with near-syncope/orthostasis. She started lisinopril 2 weeks ago. Will document and route for nurse callback.

message.subject: VA RN call: post-discharge weight check + diuretic question
message.body: Calling on behalf of Truman VA. Mr. Boateng was discharged 3 days ago after a CHF exacerbation and is local to Phelps for outpatient cardiology. Weight today is up 2 lb from discharge. Asking the Phelps team to take over diuretic titration and schedule clinic in the next 3–5 days.
